title: Rochelia echinophora
taxonomy: Rochelia echinophora is a species within the genus Rochelia, which belongs to the family Boraginaceae. The genus Rochelia is a group of flowering plants, and Boraginaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as borage or forget-me-not family. The Boraginaceae family is part of the larger clade Asteridae, which also includes other flowering plant families. Asteridae is a clade within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Rochelia echinophora is therefore classified as an angiosperm within the family Boraginaceae.

intro: Rochelia echinophora is a plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The bioactive constituents of Rochelia echinophora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
